topic: ACL main page 3 Method subsection widow and linebreak polish

defect.class: method subsection heading wrap and column widow
before_text_matches: page 3 line 001: Synthetic rendering shifts. Prior sim-to-               promises.
page 3 line 039: 3.1    Material Conversion as the
defect.reader_effect: The Method transition looked visually unstable: a long subsection title wrapped awkwardly, and one word from the MDL-semantics boundary sentence was stranded at the top of the right column.

changes.summary: Shortened the 3.1 title to Conversion as Intervention.
Compressed the MDL-semantics boundary paragraph while preserving rewrite scope, declaring-layer path behavior, fixed-value fallback, and non-preserved MDL-effect risks.

rejected_iterations.summary: Title-only rewrite moved the lone promises. from the right-column top to a one-word line at the left-column bottom.
First paragraph rewrite introduced tex-, re-, and a one-word claims. line.
Second paragraph rewrite introduced procedu- before the final accepted risk sentence.

after_evidence.targeted_scan_result: Page 3 no longer has the Method heading wrap, the promises. widow, or the rejected tex-/re-/procedu-/claims. artifacts.
after_evidence.full_scan_result: The full after scan reports only the pre-existing Related Work compound break sim-to- and normal bibliography-column hyphenation.
after_evidence.visual_result: Page 3 now enters 3.2 at the top of the right column, keeps 3.1 on one line, and ends the MDL-semantics paragraph without an orphaned word.

claim_boundary: Method prose and linebreak polish only. No metric values, evidence pools, figures, citations, metadata, or supported/forbidden claim scopes were changed.
residual_risk: Page 3 still contains the pre-existing Related Work compound break sim-to-. Reference pages retain normal bibliography-column hyphenation.
